metadata.description: State-specific denial letter requirements for auto insurance claims. Each state section details mandatory elements, regulatory references, and required disclosures that must appear in claim denial letters.
sources: California Code of Regulations Title 10, Section 2695.7
Florida Statutes Chapter 627 and FAC Rule 69O-166
New York Insurance Law Section 3420(d) and 11 NYCRR Part 216

denial_letter_requirements.description: State-mandated requirements for written claim denial letters in auto insurance.

mandatory_elements.requirement: Every denial of a claim must be in writing.
The denial letter must state ALL bases for denial. Any ground not stated is waived.
Must reference specific policy provisions, conditions, or exclusions that support the denial.
Must include notice of the right to appeal and how to submit an appeal.
Must include California Department of Insurance contact information for filing complaints.
Letter should reference the California Fair Claims Settlement Practices Regulations as governing authority.

prohibited_practices: Denying based on grounds not supported by policy language
California.notes: California requires the most detailed denial letters. The 'all grounds stated' rule means incomplete letters create coverage waiver risk.

mandatory_elements.requirement: Denial must be communicated in writing.
Must state specific grounds for denial including applicable policy provisions.
Grounds for denial not stated in the letter cannot be asserted in subsequent proceedings.
Must include Florida Department of Financial Services contact information.

prohibited_practices: Denying without stating specific policy grounds
Florida.notes: Florida requires informal conference option. PIP denials have additional requirements under FAC Rule 69O-166.

New York.primary_regulation: NY Insurance Law Section 3420(d) and 11 NYCRR Part 216

mandatory_elements.requirement: Denial must be in writing.
Letter must state all grounds for denial. Unstated grounds may be deemed waived.
Must cite the specific policy provisions, conditions, or exclusions supporting the denial.
Must inform policyholder of right to external appeal through NY DFS External Appeal Program.
Must include New York DFS contact information for complaints.

contact.agency: New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS)

prohibited_practices: Failing to disclose external appeal rights
New York.notes: New York requires external appeal notice. No-Fault/PIP denials have additional requirements under 11 NYCRR Part 65.

Texas.primary_regulation: Texas Insurance Code Chapter 542, Subchapter B

mandatory_elements.requirement: Insurer must accept or deny the claim in writing.
Must state specific grounds for denial including referenced policy provisions.
Must disclose that wrongful denial may result in 18% annual interest penalty plus attorney fees.
Must include Texas Department of Insurance contact information.
Should reference the Office of Public Insurance Counsel (OPIC) as a free consumer resource.

prohibited_practices: Delaying decision beyond reasonable time after receipt of information
Texas.notes: Texas has strict prompt payment requirements. The 18% penalty exposure makes timely and accurate denial letters critical. Appraisal rights must be disclosed if policy provides for them.

Georgia.primary_regulation: Georgia Insurance Code Title 33, Chapter 6 and O.C.G.A. §33-6-34
supporting_regulations: O.C.G.A. §33-6-34 (reasonable time to act on claims)

mandatory_elements.requirement: Insurer must affirm or deny coverage within a reasonable time after proof of loss is submitted.
Must state specific policy provisions and facts supporting the denial.
Denial letter should note that bad faith denial may result in 50% of loss plus attorney fees under O.C.G.A. §33-4-6.
Must include Georgia Office of Commissioner of Insurance contact information.

contact.agency: Georgia Office of Commissioner of Insurance (OCI)

mandatory_elements.requirement: For partial-loss claims Georgia requires diminished value to be considered. If denied, denial must address DV separately.
mandatory_elements.note: DV denial requires separate explanation and reference to the 17-C formula methodology.

prohibited_practices: Unreasonably denying a claim without factual basis (bad faith exposure)
Failing to address diminished value on applicable partial-loss claims

bad_faith_penalty.description: Bad faith denial may result in 50% of loss value plus reasonable attorney fees.

Georgia.notes: Georgia is unique in requiring diminished value consideration on partial-loss claims. Denials should explicitly address DV when applicable. The 17-C formula is the state-prescribed method.
